title: Upon Loach.
content:
Seal'd up with night-gum, Loach each morning lies,
    Till his wife licking, so unglues his eyes.
    No question then, but such a lick is sweet,
    When a warm tongue does with such ambers meet.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Loach.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
